---
title: "reference_semiring_matmul"
generated: true
description: "Pure-JAX reference for [`semiring_matmul`](../nitrix-semiring-matmul/semiring_matmul.qmd)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.semiring.matmul`](../nitrix-semiring-matmul.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.semiring._reference`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:reference_semiring_matmul -->
```python
reference_semiring_matmul(A: Num[Array, 'm k'], B: Num[Array, 'k n'], *, semiring: Semiring[S]) -> Num[Array, 'm n']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:reference_semiring_matmul -->
Pure-JAX reference for [`semiring_matmul`](../nitrix-semiring-matmul/semiring_matmul.qmd).

Computes the dense semiring matrix product
$C_{ij} = \bigoplus_k \left( A_{ik} \otimes B_{kj} \right)$,
where $\oplus$ is the monoid reduction and $\otimes$ the
per-element combine of the supplied algebra.  The contraction axis is
walked with `jax.lax.fori_loop`, folding each rank-one outer
product into the accumulator via `semiring.monoid.update`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:reference_semiring_matmul -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `A` | `Num` | `m` × `k` | — | Left operand, shape `(m, k)`. |
| `B` | `Num` | `k` × `n` | — | Right operand, shape `(k, n)`. Its leading axis is the contraction dimension shared with `A`. |
| `semiring` | `Semiring[S]` | — | — | The algebra to reduce under. Either a relaxed [`Semiring`](../nitrix-semiring/Semiring.qmd) or a [`StrictSemiring`](../nitrix-semiring/StrictSemiring.qmd); this function fixes the reduction order, so the relaxed type is sufficient. |

**Returns** `Num` — shape `m` × `n`. The product `C`, shape `(m, n)`, with the same dtype as `A`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:reference_semiring_matmul -->
::: {.callout-note}
There are no batching dimensions here.  The public
[`semiring_matmul`](../nitrix-semiring-matmul/semiring_matmul.qmd) handles broadcast over leading batch
dimensions by `vmap`-ing over this reference.  Keeping the reference
two-dimensional simplifies the Pallas parity contract.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:reference_semiring_matmul -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `m` | rows of a multi-row contrast |
| `k` | kernel or basis components |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
